sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the player (blue square with black dot) is now at the left side of the maze, near the red area. Let me check the exact position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position
after_seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in after_seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] == 24 and node['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"Player: boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Check maze structure around player
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Rows 30-42, cols 14-30 ===")
for i in range(30, 43):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][14:30]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player is at rows 32-36, cols 14-18. The red trap is at rows 38-42, cols 14-18. The player is 2 moves (12 rows) away from the red trap.
Looking at the maze structure:
- Rows 30-37, cols 20-29: White area (WWWWWWWWWW)
- Rows 38-42, cols 14-18: Red trap (RRRRR)
- Row 38, col 19: Black (B)
So the player needs to move RIGHT to enter the white area at cols 20-24 before reaching the red trap.
But earlier, RIGHT from rows 14-18, cols 14-18 didn't work. Let me try RIGHT from rows 32-36, cols 14-18.
Actually, looking at the maze structure more carefully:
- Rows 30-37, cols 14-19: Black (BBBBBB)
- Rows 30-37, cols 20-29: White (WWWWWWWWWW)
So the player is at rows 32-36, cols 14-18 (black), and the white area is at cols 20-29. The player needs to move RIGHT to enter the white area.
But the player is 5 rows tall and 4 columns wide. Moving RIGHT would put the player at cols 20-24, which overlaps with the white area. Let me try RIGHT.

sections.content:

- **Player**: At rows 32-36, cols 14-18. Red trap at rows 38-42, cols 14-18 (2 moves away).
- **White area**: At rows 30-37, cols 20-29, to the RIGHT of player.
- **Goal**: Move RIGHT into white area before hitting red trap.
Plan: Try RIGHT to enter white area.
